Title: Eudicot primary cell wall glucomannan is related in synthesis, structure, and function to xyloglucan

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: β-GGM backbone with repeating [4-Glc-β-1,4-Man-β-1] disaccharide pattern
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes the patterned glucomannan backbone containing alternating β-1,4-linked Glc and Man residues that forms β-GGM
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies the structural backbone directly required for target product formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Primary cell wall hemicellulose involved in cell expansion, cellulose organization, and partially redundant wall function with xyloglucan
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Primary cell wall-rich tissues including etiolated seedlings, young stem, leaves, siliques, naked seeds, and seed mucilage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for synthesis of patterned β-GGM; csla2 removes CSLA2-dependent β-GGM and, in xxt1 xxt2 background, loss of CSLA2 exacerbates dwarfism, shorter siliques, shorter hypocotyls, reduced cell elongation, and altered cellulose organization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: knockout mutant analysis; double/triple mutant genetic interaction; PACE oligosaccharide profiling; glycosidase digestion; microscopy; ssNMR-supported wall polymer assignment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Inferred from presence of CSLA2-dependent products in multiple primary cell wall-rich tissues; co-expressed with MAGT1, MSR1, and MBGT1
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Etiolated seedling growth; dark-grown hypocotyl assays; callus culture for ssNMR
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Single mutant has only mild growth effects, but loss in XyG-deficient background causes stronger growth and cell elongation defects
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering primary cell wall glucomannan architecture, but effects should be evaluated together with XyG pathway genes because of functional redundancy
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CSLA2-dependent β-GGM differs from CSLA9-dependent acetylated/random glucomannan; CSLA2 products are absent or strongly reduced in magt1-derived β-GGM branch fingerprints
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Phylogenetic analysis; co-expression analysis using ATTED-II
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CSLA2 is necessary for synthesis of the major patterned primary-wall glucomannan β-GGM in Arabidopsis. 2. CSLA2 generates a repeating Glc-Man backbone that underlies α-galactosylated and β-Gal-α-Gal decorated β-GGM. 3. Loss of CSLA2 alone causes mild phenotypes, but in xxt1 xxt2 plants it strongly aggravates defects in hypocotyl elongation, plant height, silique length, and cellulose wall organization, showing functional overlap with xyloglucan.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Glucomannan biosynthesis in primary/secondary wall-associated tissues
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: CSLA9-dependent glucomannan with relatively random Glc distribution, consistent with acetylated galactoglucomannan-like structure
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes a distinct glucomannan backbone whose mannanase products indicate a more randomly distributed Glc-Man pattern than β-GGM
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms an alternative glucomannan product rather than the focal β-GGM end product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall hemicellulose distinct from β-GGM
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Primary cell wall-rich tissues, especially leaves where CSLA9-dependent products predominate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Necessary for synthesis of most longer CSLA9-dependent glucomannan oligosaccharides; distinct from CSLA2-dependent β-GGM
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: knockout mutant analysis; PACE oligosaccharide profiling; glycosidase digestion
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected functionally across several tissues and dominant in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Analysis in etiolated seedlings, young stem, leaves, siliques, and naked seeds
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be used to modulate glucomannan composition across tissues, especially leaf-enriched glucomannan
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper treats CSLA9 mainly as synthesizing a different glucomannan type analogous to previously described AcGGM; not the principal β-GGM synthase
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CSLA9 synthesizes a glucomannan distinct from CSLA2-dependent β-GGM. 2. Its products are consistent with a less regularly patterned, AcGGM-like glucomannan. 3. CSLA9-dependent glucomannan is widespread in Arabidopsis primary-wall-rich tissues and is relatively prominent in leaves.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: midstream; side-chain addition after backbone synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers α-Gal to C-6 of Man residues in the patterned β-GGM backbone, creating A units that can be further β-galactosylated
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Primary cell wall hemicellulose involved in normal cell wall function and cell expansion
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Primary cell wall-rich tissues and seed mucilage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for α-galactosylation of CSLA2-derived β-GGM; magt1 lacks the characteristic S1-S4 β-GGM oligosaccharides and accumulates simpler GM/GMGM products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: knockout mutant analysis; PACE oligosaccharide profiling; sequential glycosidase digestion
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Co-expressed with CSLA2, MSR1, and MBGT1
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Previously associated with cellulose-ray defects in seed mucilage per cited work; no new whole-plant penalty shown here
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important target to alter β-GGM substitution pattern rather than backbone abundance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MAGT1 acts before MBGT1 because β-1,2-Gal is added onto the α-1,6-Gal branch
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MAGT1 is required for α-1,6-galactosylation of CSLA2-derived β-GGM. 2. In magt1 mutants, diagnostic β-GGM oligosaccharides disappear and simpler unsubstituted backbone fragments accumulate. 3. MAGT1-generated α-Gal branches create the substrate for further β-galactosylation by MBGT1.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Arabidopsis Col-0; N. benthamiana laboratory plants
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CAZy GT47 clade A glycosyltransferase (AtGT14/group VII)
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: β-1,2-Gal-α-1,6-Gal disaccharide side chains on β-GGM (B units)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers β-Gal onto the α-Gal side chain of β-GGM, forming a β-1,2-Gal-α-1,6-Gal disaccharide branch
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Primary cell wall hemicellulose linked to cell expansion and cellulose organization; β-Gal side chains contribute to normal function especially when XyG galactosylation is compromised
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Primary cell walls and seed mucilage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for β-galactosylation of β-GGM; mbgt1 mutants lack β-galactosylated β-GGM oligosaccharides and, when combined with mur3 alleles, show enhanced dwarfing and leaf/stem defects
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-expression analysis; knockout mutant analysis in two alleles; in vitro galactosyltransferase assay using transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana microsomes; PACE product analysis; genetic interaction with mur3
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Co-expressed with CSLA2, MAGT1, and MSR1 in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: microsome/membrane fraction inferred from activity assay and immunoblot of recombinant protein
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assays required UDP-Gal; transient heterologous expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for transient expression in N. benthamiana; native endogenous context in Arabidopsis mutants
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Single mbgt1 plants are phenotypically near wild type, but loss of MBGT1 worsens mur3 growth defects, indicating context-dependent penalties when combined with XyG side-chain defects
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering β-GGM fine structure and wall mechanics; should be considered together with XyG side-chain genes due to functional redundancy
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified from GT47-A group VII phylogeny and ATTED-II co-expression; this is the paper's key newly identified enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Phylogenomics of GT47-A across 96 streptophytes; co-expression analysis using ATTED-II
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. At4g13990/MBGT1 encodes the β-GGM β-galactosyltransferase that forms β-1,2-Gal on α-Gal side chains. 2. Two independent mbgt1 knockout lines completely lack β-galactosylated β-GGM oligosaccharides. 3. Recombinant MBGT1 confers UDP-Gal-dependent β-galactosyltransferase activity in vitro on β-GGM acceptors. 4. MBGT1-dependent side-chain elaboration is biologically important because its loss enhances mur3 xyloglucan mutant phenotypes.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: xyloglucan side chains lacking third-position galactose; UDP-Gal
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: β-galactosylated xyloglucan side chains (L/F-containing XyG units)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Adds β-Gal to specific xyloglucan side chains, generating normal galactosylated XyG subunits
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major primary cell wall hemicellulose important for normal growth, cell wall mechanics, and cell expansion
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: mur3 mutants have defective XyG galactosylation and growth abnormalities; combining mur3 with mbgt1 further aggravates rosette curling, dwarfing, and branch/stem phenotypes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: genetic mutant analysis; double mutant construction with mbgt1; XyG oligosaccharide profiling by PACE
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Growth phenotypes evaluated in standard chamber conditions
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes strong developmental defects including curled rosettes and short stems in mur3-3; defects are worsened by simultaneous loss of β-GGM β-galactosylation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Highlights need to preserve XyG side-chain galactosylation when altering β-GGM side chains in wall engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core comparative pathway gene because direct double-mutant experiments establish functional interaction with MBGT1-dependent β-GGM
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MUR3-mediated XyG β-galactosylation is important for normal Arabidopsis growth. 2. Loss of MUR3 creates XyG side-chain defects and visible growth phenotypes. 3. Combining mur3 with mbgt1 causes stronger defects than mur3 alone, showing analogous functional importance of β-GGM and XyG disaccharide side chains.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: early/midstream side-chain addition to XyG backbone
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Adds α-1,6-xylosyl residues to the xyloglucan backbone as part of XyG biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Primary cell wall hemicellulose important for plant growth, cell elongation, and cellulose organization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Together with XXT2 required for detectable XyG; csla2 xxt1 xxt2 triple mutants show more severe growth, silique, hypocotyl, cell elongation, and cellulose wall defects than xxt1 xxt2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: double/triple mutant genetic analysis; PACE confirmation of XyG loss; hypocotyl and plant phenotyping; cryo-SEM; confocal cellulose staining
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Etiolated hypocotyl assays under dark growth
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss with XXT2 reduces growth and cell elongation; combined loss with β-GGM pathway causes stronger defects
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Wall engineering strategies affecting β-GGM should consider XXT-mediated XyG redundancy to avoid compounded growth defects
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This paper evaluates XXT1 mainly in combination with XXT2 as the canonical XyG-deficient background
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. XXT1, together with XXT2, is essential for normal XyG biosynthesis. 2. Loss of XXT1/XXT2 creates a growth-defective XyG-null background. 3. Additional loss of CSLA2-dependent β-GGM in this background causes stronger developmental and cell wall defects, revealing functional overlap between the two hemicelluloses.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: early/midstream side-chain addition to XyG backbone
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Adds α-1,6-xylosyl residues to the xyloglucan backbone as part of XyG biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Primary cell wall hemicellulose important for plant growth, cell elongation, and cellulose organization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Together with XXT1 required for detectable XyG; csla2 xxt1 xxt2 triple mutants show more severe growth, silique, hypocotyl, cell elongation, and cellulose wall defects than xxt1 xxt2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: double/triple mutant genetic analysis; PACE confirmation of XyG loss; hypocotyl and plant phenotyping; cryo-SEM; confocal cellulose staining
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Etiolated hypocotyl assays under dark growth
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss with XXT1 reduces growth and cell elongation; combined loss with β-GGM pathway causes stronger defects
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Same as XXT1; maintain XyG biosynthesis when modifying β-GGM to avoid severe wall defects
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Analyzed as part of the xxt1 xxt2 background rather than separately
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. XXT2 works with XXT1 in essential XyG biosynthesis. 2. XyG deficiency in xxt1 xxt2 exposes the developmental contribution of β-GGM. 3. Combined loss of XXT2-containing XyG biosynthesis and CSLA2-dependent β-GGM leads to stronger wall and growth defects than loss of XyG alone.
